title: Workspace Folders
subtitle: games, templates, and features
description: Repository workspace roots for reusable templates, copyable feature packs, and independent game folders. The map shows ownership boundaries here, not implementation files inside games, templates, or features.
role: game, template, and feature workspace roots

children.title: templates/
children.description: Container for reusable game template folders. Immediate child folders are explicit map nodes; files inside each template remain template-owned implementation detail.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Templates workspace entrypoint: explains reusable template folders, template registry, game-copy ownership, and new template creation command.

children.title: templates.json
children.description: Template workspace registry data. Asset Viewer and backlog source helpers read it, but templates/ owns the file.

children.title: template/
children.description: Default reusable game template folder. The map stops at this folder; source files inside are template-owned implementation detail.

children.title: TEMPLATE.md
children.description: Template copy model: what is shared, what is copied into games, feature-pack policy, and game-only boundaries.

children.title: new_template.mjs
children.description: Copies an existing template into templates/<template-id>/, registers it as a reusable template asset root, creates or reuses the matching Taskboard project, and refreshes generated VS Code tasks/launch entries.

children.title: new_template.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests proving new_template copies template files, registers template asset roots, creates a Taskboard project, and refreshes generated VS Code project entries without touching the real repository.

children.title: features/
children.description: Feature group for reusable copyable feature-pack folders. Immediate child folders are explicit map nodes; files inside each feature remain feature-owned implementation detail.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Features workspace entrypoint: explains copyable feature-pack shape, rules, and future manifest/install possibilities.

children.title: game-state/
children.description: Reusable schema-first game-state feature pack with code generation scripts, state contract references, migration rules, and DevAPI adapter generation.

children.title: nt-game-state-management
children.description: Agent-facing skill router for the Game State feature pack. Canonical scripts and references live in features/game-state/.

children.title: games/
children.description: Container for independent game folders. Immediate child folders are explicit map nodes; files inside each game remain game-owned implementation detail.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Games workspace entrypoint: explains independent game folders, game asset registry, private design knowledge, and game creation command.

children.title: games.json
children.description: Game workspace registry data for game-local asset roots. Asset Viewer and backlog source helpers read it, but games/ owns the file.

children.title: new_game.mjs
children.description: Copies templates/<template-id>/ into games/<game-id>/, registers that game asset root through backlog source helpers, creates or reuses the matching Taskboard project, and refreshes generated VS Code tasks/launch entries.

children.title: new_game.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests proving new_game copies template files, registers game asset roots, creates a Taskboard project, and refreshes generated VS Code project entries without touching the real repository.
